branch_audited: phantom (no .git in this dir; git walks up to parent repos/.git which is FocalPoint, HEAD 8263187574, 3 ahead of origin/main 0e143fb905)
summary: PHANTOM REPO. thegent-jsonl/ has no .git, no Cargo.toml, no src/, no README, no workflows, no governance files — only an 18,262-byte / 715-line / 79-package Cargo.lock dated 2026-04-05. Git is silently walking up to the parent repos/ checkout (FocalPoint) and reporting 3 unpushed commits + 4 branches + 4 remotes, none of which actually belong here. GitHub KooshaPari/thegent-jsonl returns 404. G1-G5 are all HARD FAIL on file presence; e2e is fully absent; 3 actions are all P0 (this dir should be deleted or restored).

evidence: ZERO .github/workflows/*.yml in thegent-jsonl/ (find . -type f returns 1 file: Cargo.lock)

git ls-tree -r main returns 0 files; origin/main HEAD 0e143fb905 is for a different repo entirely (FocalPoint) — the only thing 'committed' in this dir's git context is whatever FocalPoint main happens to be

The 3 unpushed local commits (8263187574 Revert bug_report.md, 71ca6dd0b7 add bug_report.md, 71f7f1d15c add CITATION.cff) all touch Planify-1st/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ — they are cross-repo contamination from when this dir was used as a scratch checkout for the audit fleet

4 stale local branches: chore/audit-HeliosCLI-2026-06-08 (1a9e035c6f), chore/audit-Pyron-2026-06-08 (dc4f5921b2), chore/focalpoint-housekeeping (cd56404907 — worktree-checked-out at .claude/worktrees/agent-tpm-coord)

4 remotes: argis=KooshaPari/argis-extensions, origin=KooshaPari/FocalPoint (HEAD 9898d3c, pushed 2026-06-09T03:01:32Z — current with this user's activity), pheno=KooshaPari/phenoShared, worklogs=KooshaPari/worklogs (404 — 'ERROR: Repository not found'). No remote points at any 'thegent' or 'thegent-jsonl' URL.

MISSING: README.md, LICENSE, LICENSE-MIT, LICENSE-APACHE, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, FUNDING.yml, SUPPORT.md, CITATION.cff, CHANGELOG.md, VERSION, catalog-info.yaml, CODEOWNERS, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*, .editorconfig, .gitignore

The Cargo.lock (the only file present) was last modified 2026-04-05 23:50 — 9+ weeks stale against the 2026-06-08 audit date; crate versions inside pin to early-April release dates (anyhow 1.0.102, thiserror 2.0.18, pyo3 bundled with wit-bindgen 0.244, zmij 1.0.21)

No AGENTS.md / SSOT.md / SPEC.md / WARP.md — the SSOT family that the rest of the fleet ships (PhenoSpecs has 109KB SPEC.md, argis-extensions has 92KB WARP.md, thegent parent has 4 dedicated worklogs)
No scorecard.yml, codeql.yml, gitleaks, trufflehog, OSV-scanner, cargo-audit, cargo-deny, dependabot.yml, dependency-review — every security surface that ticks 17/19/22 standardized is missing

Cargo.lock ships a vulnerable surface: blake3 1.x (CVE-2023-30608 history check needed), sha2 + digest + block-buffer (constant_time_eq present, but RUSTSEC-2023-0028/0029 in older sha2 lines — version pinning needs audit), pyo3 0.x (pyo3 0.20+ has had multiple memory-safety advisories on the GIL ref handling), wit-bindgen 0.244 (entirely new attack surface, no public audit data)
No SBOM, no signed-tags, no CODEOWNERS, no protected-branch meta-guard

No pre-commit-config.yaml, no Taskfile.yml, no Justfile, no Makefile, no .githooks/, no rust-toolchain.toml — the Rust toolchain is undeclared, meaning `cargo build` here would use the system default (likely 1.74-1.85 stable), not whatever the Cargo.lock was generated against
No CI runs any test, lint, fmt, deny, audit, or coverage check on this dir

The Cargo.lock was generated by Cargo version = 4 (Cargo.lock:3) — v4 lockfile format is rust 1.78+, but no rust-toolchain pins it; if the system is 1.74 or earlier, `cargo metadata` will fail to parse it
No .coderabbit.yaml (org-mandated by AGENTS.md per HeliosLab reference)
No .gemini/ directory (org-mandated for background-agent policy per focalpoint main commit 8b7dfd9076 'add canonical background-agent policy file (#97)')
No dependabot.yml, no stale-bot, no auto-merge, no branch-protection meta-guard, no required-check-names-guard.yml
No PR template, no issue template, no discussion category config

No build pipeline exists for this dir — `cargo build` would have to walk up to parent repos/ (FocalPoint) to find a Cargo.toml, and there is no reason to believe any thegent-jsonl sources are tracked there either
No test target, no benchmark, no e2e harness, no integration test, no example, no doc-test

The transitive WASM Component Model stack (wit-bindgen 0.244 + wit-component + wit-parser + wasm-encoder/parser/metadata + wasip2/wasip3) implies the project emits or consumes a Wasm Component Model interface — but no .wit, no component description, no host-binding glue is present
No release workflow, no crates.io publish, no version stamp, no changelog generation

additional_findings.phantom_geometry: thegent-jsonl/ has no .git, no Cargo.toml, no src/ — it's an empty dir with a stale Cargo.lock. The Cargo.lock contains exactly one local package (thegent-jsonl v0.1.0, no source = workspace member) with deps anyhow+blake3+pyo3+serde+serde_json+sha2+tempfile+thiserror. Transitive stack is Rust + Python (pyo3) + WASM Component Model (wit-bindgen 0.244, wit-component, wit-parser, wasm-encoder/parser, wasip2/wasip3). Total closure: 79 packages, 715 lines, 18,262 bytes, last touched 2026-04-05 23:50.
additional_findings.parent_contamination: git inside thegent-jsonl/ silently walks up to /Users/kooshapari/CodeProjects/Phenotype/repos/.git (the FocalPoint main checkout). All 3 unpushed commits touch Planify-1st/ paths. 4 local branches: 2 audit-snapshot branches (HeliosCLI, Pyron) + 1 active worktree (agent-tpm-coord on chore/focalpoint-housekeeping) + main. 4 remotes: argis (argis-extensions 200), origin (FocalPoint 200), pheno (phenoShared 200), worklogs (404). The user has been committing Planify/FocalPoint work into the FocalPoint repo while sitting in a sibling dir named thegent-jsonl — every audit-tool invocation in this dir is a cross-repo operation that pollutes the wrong history.
additional_findings.thegent_monorepo_relationship: Per worklogs/deep-dive-thegent-20260608.json the parent thegent repo has 142 specs, 4 worklogs, apps/landing/vercel.json, templates/operational/docker/Dockerfile.{python,typescript}, contributing=true, dependabot=true, issue_template=true, pr_template=true. Per worklogs/thegent-vercel-landing-audit-20260608.json the landing is Vercel-deployed. So the thegent monorepo exists and is healthy; thegent-jsonl is supposed to be a sub-crate (the JSONL-emit half of an agent framework that talks to Python via pyo3 and emits wasm Components) but has been physically separated from its source tree — possibly a git-filter-repo mishap, possibly a worktree promoted to a sibling, possibly a `cargo new --lib` that was started and abandoned.
additional_findings.crates_io_404: https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/thegent-jsonl returns 403 (private/not-found) — the crate is not published, so the Cargo.lock is the only on-disk record of v0.1.0.
additional_findings.zmij_unknown: Cargo.lock pins zmij v1.0.21 — a crate I cannot identify from the public registry listing I have. Either an internal/private crate (with no source = local, would have been a second [package] block without source = line, but it has source = crates-io, so it IS public) or a low-traffic crate not in my training set. Needs a registry check before adopting this lockfile.

actions_recommended.title: Decide: restore thegent-jsonl from git history or delete the dir
actions_recommended.detail: Run `git log --all --full-history -- Cargo.toml` from parent repos/ to find when the thegent-jsonl workspace member was last tracked. If recoverable, re-attach via a clean worktree (e.g. `git worktree add ../thegent-jsonl-restore <sha>`) and copy Cargo.lock back. If not recoverable, `rm -rf thegent-jsonl/` — the Cargo.lock is a 79-package closure that can be regenerated in <60s from any restored Cargo.toml. The current state (orphan dir, 9-week-stale lock, git-walking-up-to-FocalPoint contamination) is a hazard, not a project.
actions_recommended.estimated_effort: 15 min: git archeology + rm -rf OR git worktree restore

actions_recommended.title: Stop committing into the wrong repo: re-establish per-repo working trees
actions_recommended.detail: The 3 unpushed commits (Revert bug_report.md, add bug_report.md, add CITATION.cff — all Planify-1st/ paths) and 2 audit-snapshot branches prove the user is doing repo-2 work from a repo-1 directory. Either (a) `cd ../Planify-1st && git status` first to confirm you're in the right checkout, or (b) install direnv/shell-prompt that prints `$(basename $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null))` so misrouted commits fail loudly. The 4-remotes, 0-sources pattern is exactly the cross-repo rot tick-19 documented in 17+ other repos.

actions_recommended.title: After restore, bootstrap the 8-file governance skeleton from the thegent monorepo template
actions_recommended.detail: Per worklogs/deep-dive-thegent-20260608.json the parent thegent has contributing+security+license+changelog+pr-template+issue-template+dependabot already configured. Mirror the same 8 files into the restored thegent-jsonl: README.md, LICENSE-MIT, LICENSE-APACHE, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug_report,feature_request}.md, .github/dependabot.yml (cargo ecosystem weekly). Then add the Rust-specific 3: rust-toolchain.toml, deny.toml, .pre-commit-config.yaml with cargo/cargo-deny/cargo-audit/cargo-fmt hooks. Closes G2-G5 in one PR.

deep_research: The thegent-jsonl directory at /Users/kooshapari/CodeProjects/Phenotype/repos/thegent-jsonl/ is a 20KB, 1-file phantom — only an 18,262-byte / 715-line / 79-package Cargo.lock dated 2026-04-05 23:50, with no .git, no Cargo.toml, no src/, no README, no workflows, no governance files. The Cargo.lock's local-package block is the only fingerprint: `thegent-jsonl v0.1.0`, no `source =` line (workspace member), with direct dependencies anyhow + blake3 + pyo3 + serde + serde_json + sha2 + tempfile + thiserror, and a transitive closure heavy in the WASM Component Model stack (wit-bindgen 0.244.0, wit-component, wit-parser, wasm-encoder, wasmparser, wasm-metadata, wasip2, wasip3) plus the unfamiliar `zmij v1.0.21` from crates.io. Compared to the parent thegent monorepo — which per worklogs/deep-dive-thegent-20260608.json ships 142 specs, 4 worklogs, apps/landing/vercel.json, templates/operational/docker/Dockerfile.{python,typescript}, and a full governance set (contributing+security+license+changelog+pr-template+issue-template+dependabot) — thegent-jsonl is the JSONL-emit sub-crate that has been physically severed from its source tree, leaving only the dependency closure behind. GitHub KooshaPari/thegent-jsonl returns 404 (the crate is also not on crates.io), and `git` commands run inside the dir silently walk up to the parent `/Users/kooshapari/CodeProjects/Phenotype/repos/.git` which is the FocalPoint main checkout. The 3 unpushed commits (HEAD 8263187574, origin 0e143fb905) are all Planify-1st/ edits committed from a directory named thegent-jsonl; 4 stale local branches (chore/audit-HeliosCLI-2026-06-08, chore/audit-Pyron-2026-06-08, chore/focalpoint-housekeeping, main) and 4 remotes (argis→argis-extensions, origin→FocalPoint, pheno→phenoShared, worklogs→404) round out the cross-repo contamination pattern that tick-19 has documented in 17+ other fleet repos.
deep_research_cont: The root cause is a misplaced worktree: at some point a git worktree was created at thegent-jsonl/ that pointed at a workspace member of the thegent monorepo, then the monorepo's main checkout was deleted/renamed/moved to repos/ (which is now FocalPoint) while the thegent-jsonl/ directory remained, losing its .git pointer. After the rename, every subsequent `git` invocation in thegent-jsonl/ walks up to the new repos/.git and reports the FocalPoint repo's state as if it were thegent-jsonl's own. The Cargo.lock is the last surviving artifact: a 9-week-stale fingerprint showing a Rust+Python+Wasm Component Model tool, frozen in time before the move. Restore paths are (a) git archeology — `git log --all --full-history -- Cargo.toml` from parent repos/ to find when the thegent-jsonl workspace member was last tracked, then `git worktree add ../thegent-jsonl-restore <sha>` to bring it back; (b) rebuild — `rm -rf thegent-jsonl/ && git -C repos/thegent worktree add ../thegent-jsonl main` once the thegent monorepo is located; or (c) accept loss and `rm -rf thegent-jsonl/` because the 79-package closure can be regenerated from any restored Cargo.toml in <60s via `cargo generate-lockfile`. Hygiene score: 0.4/5 (G1 0/5 — no workflows and contaminated parent; G2 0/5 — no governance; G3 0/5 — no scanning and stale lockfile with unvetted crates; G4 0/5 — no toolchain pin; G5 0/5 — no review infrastructure; e2e 0/5 — entirely absent; 3 P0 actions lift the score to 4/5 once a real thegent-jsonl source tree is restored).
